2603.15295.title: Datasets for Verb Alternations across Languages: BLM Templates and Data Augmentation Strategies
2603.15295.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable performance across various sentence-based linguistic phenomena, yet their ability to capture cross-sentence paradigmatic patterns, such as verb alternations, remains underexplored. In this work, we present curated paradigm-based datasets for four languages, designed to probe systematic cross-sentence knowledge of verb alternations (change-of-state and object-drop constructions in English, German and Italian, and Hebrew binyanim). The datasets comprise thousands of the Blackbird Language Matrices (BLMs) problems. The BLM task -- an RPM/ARC-like task devised specifically for language -- is a controlled linguistic puzzle where models must select the sentence that completes a pattern according to syntactic and semantic rules. We introduce three types of templates varying in complexity and apply linguistically-informed data augmentation strategies across synthetic and natural data. We provide simple baseline performance results across English, Italian, German, and Hebrew, that demonstrate the diagnostic usefulness of the datasets.
2603.15295.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在各種基於句子的語言現象中表現出卓越的性能，但它們捕捉跨句子範式模式的能力，例如動詞交替，仍然未被充分探索。在這項工作中，我們提出了針對四種語言的精心策劃的範式數據集，旨在探究動詞交替的系統性跨句子知識（英語、德語和意大利語中的狀態變化和物件省略結構，以及希伯來語的binyanim）。這些數據集包含數千個黑鳥語言矩陣（BLMs）問題。BLM任務——一種專門為語言設計的類似RPM/ARC的任務——是一種受控的語言謎題，模型必須根據語法和語義規則選擇完成模式的句子。我們引入了三種類型的模板，複雜性各異，並在合成和自然數據中應用語言學知識驅動的數據增強策略。我們提供了英語、意大利語、德語和希伯來語的簡單基線性能結果，顯示了這些數據集的診斷實用性。
2603.15295.author: Giuseppe Samo et.al.
